skills: sign/notice comprehension (directional arrow, exhibition hall signage)
pie-chart interpretation from a descriptive passage (comparative quantifiers: most/more than/few)
comparative data-table selection matching a comparative sentence (taller than X, shorter than Y)
picture-to-definition matching (identify a sport/object from a spoken description)
ditransitive verb word-order correctness (gave X to Y)
culture knowledge (Thai Wai Khru / Teacher's Day ceremony)
postcard reading comprehension (main idea, time detail, day detail, character identification, location inference)
dialogue completion across many everyday settings (22 items, items 19-40)
grammar: ditransitive verb order (gave the flowers to the teacher)
modal verbs (should, could, would, may, can)
question_types: pie-chart selection from a descriptive passage
postcard reading with 5 linked comprehension questions
short 2-line dialogue completion across school/phone/shop/restaurant/family settings (22 items)
reading_patterns: first-person postcard to a parent (~90-110 words) -> questions on main idea, specific time detail, specific day detail, identifying a named character's relationship (inferred from affectionate closing line), and inferring the country/location from cultural/food/language clues (not stated directly)
dialogue_patterns: sympathy for minor ailment (headache) leading to an offer of help
wh-question matched to an ordinal/numeric answer format (time, price, quantity, floor)
imperative/request exchange resolved by a logical action (turn on the light)
contradiction-logic exchange (rejecting a suggestion reveals the implied question)
opinion-elicitation exchange (street food) vs fact-elicitation exchange, distinguished by required answer type
weather-conditional exchange (bring umbrella because it will rain)
restaurant preference exchange resolved by an exclusion clue ("they only serve Thai food" -> want the non-Thai option)
sympathy for physical exhaustion -> suggestion to rest
offering help to a stranger/foreigner looking for someone
distractor_patterns: wrong adjective/word order among otherwise-plausible sentences
swapped ditransitive argument order (gave the students the teacher / gave the teacher to the flowers)
mismatched holiday symbol for the correct greeting phrase
comparative-direction reversed in table items (taller/shorter swapped)
wrong-register reply lifted from a different topic (e.g. crediting a compliment about a shirt when a dress was asked about)
exclusion-logic trap: distractor belongs to the excluded category stated in the prompt (Thai/Asian food only -> Thai-sounding distractor is wrong)
restates the question instead of answering it
answer_logic: postcard/diary items require inferring unstated facts (country, relationship) from indirect cultural/linguistic/food clues rather than an explicit statement
exclusion-based dialogue items require identifying the one option that does NOT belong to a category explicitly ruled out in the prompt
picture-definition items require matching a spoken description's defining features (equipment, actions) to the correct picture, not surface keywords
comparative table/data items require correct directionality across at least two named entities
notes: Extracted via direct visual reading of the scanned exam (pdftotext/tesseract unavailable in this environment; PyMuPDF page renders were read directly, no OCR binary used). Cover page printed exam date is 1 Feb 2563 (BE+1 sitting-date convention, consistent across all scanned years) but the file/manifest year label 2562 (academic year) is retained per NIETS convention. 40 items total, items 1-18 standalone/reading, items 19-40 dialogue completion (22 items) — a notably larger dialogue-completion share than 2558-2561.
